title: senecio alberti-smithii
intro: Senecio alberti-smithii, a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, is also known as Albert Smith's ragwort. It is a perennial plant native to New Zealand, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and flowers. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may provide health benefits such as wound healing, reduced pain, and improved immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of these preparations.
